name: Lower Navarrese Basque

ancestors.notes: Lower Navarrese inherits the standard Basque grapheme base and is typologically intermediate, sharing aspiration features with Souletin while retaining core Batua consonantism.

notes: Lower Navarrese (Behe-Nafar). Spoken in Basse-Navarre (Pyrénées-Atlantiques, France), a French Basque Country territory. Phonologically intermediate between the southern Spanish-side dialects and the eastern Souletin variety. Shares aspirated stop allophones [pʰ, tʰ, kʰ] with Souletin but these are less systematic, occurring primarily in syllable onset before stressed vowels. Aspiration (/h/) is fully productive. French contact influence is present but more moderate than in Souletin. Often grouped with Lapurdian and Souletin as a northeastern Basque dialect cluster.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
